figures.caption: Fig. 4. MoreQualitative comparisons on artifact removal.

Fig. 5. Same-scene comparison under varying input views on the bicycle scene of Mip-NeRF360 [Barron et al. 2022]. We fix the same target novel view and compare DIFIX3D+ and GeoQuery under 3, 6, and 9 training views. GeoQuery maintains more stable reconstruction quality as view sparsity increases.
Fig. 6. MoreQualitative results on Mip-NeRF360 dataset [Barron et al. 2022].
Fig. 7. MoreQualitative results on DL3DV-Benchmark dataset [Ling et al. 2024].

Table 2. Quantitative comparison of rendering quality on Mip-NeRF360 [Barron et al. 2022] and DL3DV-Benchmark [Ling et al. 2024] under different numbers of input views (3, 6, and 9 views). ‡ denotes results reproduced using the official implementations of the corresponding methods.

Fig. 2. Overview of GeoQuery. Starting from a sparse training set, we optimize a 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) representation and progressively refine it through iterative rendering and supervision updates. At each step, 3DGS produces an artifact-prone rendering 𝐼𝑡 . We estimate metric depth to construct a geometric correspondence field, which is used to retrieve proxy features from the reference view. The proposed Geometry-Guided Cross-View Attention (GCA) restricts retrieval to a local 𝑘 × 𝑘 neighborhood around the indexed correspondence, and an adaptive fusion module integrates the geometry-guided features into the diffusion backbone. The restored output 𝐼𝑡 is then used as a pseudo-observation for subsequent 3DGS refinement.

Fig. 9. Visual comparisons on Mip-NeRF360 dataset and DL3DV dataset between our GeoQuery and baseline methods, including 3DGS [Kerbl et al. 2023], FSGS [Zhu et al. 2024], and DIFIX3D+ [Wu et al. 2025b]. Compared to the baselines, our method produces more reliable renderings.

Fig. 3. Qualitative comparisons on artifact removal. From left to right: the artifact-corrupted 3DGS rendering, DIFIX3D+ [Wu et al. 2025b], our GeoQuery, and the ground truth. The examples illustrate that GeoQuery can more reliably restore rendering structure, producing results that are visually closer to the ground truth.
